item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might be necroposting but;
1)This is not an "Origin of SCP" sort-of story
2)This is the beginning part of a Canon on it's own
3)Not every SCP Origin story has to be a 001 Proposal
4)Not every 001 Proposal has to be an SCP Origin story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I'm way late to this one, but I'm downvoting because the description doesn't make any sense. The first sentence is word salad (like, you've boiled down a bunch of concepts to two words each and pushed on, and that does NOT work), and the rest doesn't make it any easier to visualize or understand.

The infection logs have some good imagery, but aren't particularly cohesive and aren't really enough to carry this in the random-object-generator sense. The discovery and global repercussions was the most interesting part, but didn't bring me back to a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly just the fact that there is an explanation for the custom designation is enough for me to upvote. I absolutely despise people who give SCP's special designations with no explanation for what they mean.

So thank you, you magnificent writer

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, about time this happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To the contrary, let's hope it is. It's about time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this an SCP origin story? If so it should be an SCP-001 proposal I think. But I'm not sure if it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip reminds me of Annihilation except it's way more interesting. Bravo! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making awesome mutations horrifying and gripping to read about all in one-go. Superb skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on +100!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if I can state my opinion clearly. Sorry for my English..

Apart from the hub and the tale series, the article itself just looks like something that makes people and animals mutate and gain supernatural power. I'm not saying this is written before.. but I didn't find anything that seems "new" to me.

Perhaps I am missing something important? The fact that it has a strange looking or it is based on thaumaturgy does not make the skip stand out. If the author wants to tell something like "magic is a gift and people will eventually accept it", then this concept is not elaborated in the skip, and as a result it is not clear or convincing enough.

downvoted:( sorry..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added navigation links to the rest of our team's series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The point of technical jargon is that you don't have to define everything anytime you want to talk about it.

Also usually technical details are boring to read and difficult to do in a way that doesn't break suspension of disbelief. If I told you (for example) that a type four is characterized by a vibrational frequency of 3.98 kHz and an average charge of -0.34 microcoulombs is your enjoyment meaningfully enhanced?

Using plausible sounding jargon allows authors to provide verisimilitude without getting into dubious detailed imaginary science.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to somewhat agree with Homeless Heterotroph. Some of the diction makes this a bit of a confusing read. A Category 4 extradimensional entity? What is a Category 4? What purpose does that serve to tell me? A lot of things in this article could use a footnote.

I still managed to understand this article enough to enjoy it and it was quite an interesting read. I just wish there was a bit of elaboration on somethings here and there. Oh well.

item.title: Re: Down-vote, and here's why.
item.content:encoded:
This is a very silly way to present a review, so I'm going to repost it here.

The above review.
– hide block

“I don’t speak this language dammit!”

The Article dances around reader with it’s own self selected terminology, anti-exposition, technobabble, what-have-you, it’s not something i can look at and say “Ah! Yes! I understand what the author wants me to know.”
Take for example this excerpt from the article

“Category 4 extradimensional entity which exists as a gestalt of physically and metaphysically mutagenic symbiotes”

  • Usually, it is in an author's interest to provide the readers a quick summary of what “Category XYZ” is, take for example, SCP-3001 “Red Reality” by Oz, Ouroboros:

“accessible through the creation of a momentary Class-C "Broken Entry" Wormhole. (Footnote) “A previously hypothetical type of wormhole that does not transport matter to the expected location, or has a spacetime flaw that may randomly and dangerously eject matter mid-travel.”

  • While the expectation of an SCP’s description is that it is as formal, direct, and sophisticated as possible, it is, at heart, meant to be read by individuals with an interest in science fiction regardless of their own vocabulary.
  • While it is understandable that the author would use the term “Mutagenic symbiotes” as that IS the best way of describing the aforementioned, it is technobabble. An while normally that's fine, this is technobabble Prefaced with technobabble. I don’t even have the slightest clue as a reader what i’m to make of a part physical part metaphysical anything. And i also don’t think that's the correct usage of the word “Gestalt”. (Not that i know what the is, the google-dictionary summary just makes it more confusing)

I have opted not to read any further into this article, as i feel it already warrants a downvote. Nothing, regardless of how amazing, at this point can change my mind.

Written in 23 Minutes by Homeless Heterotroph, Saturday may 19th.1

I some strong issues with this review—such as the fact that you insisted on (incorrectly) criticizing the use of the word "gestalt" while also admitting that you don't even know what that word means—but since you have made it patently clear that you have no interest in hearing anyone else's thoughts, I'll stop here.

Footnotes
1. This is part of the review.

item.title: Down-vote, and here's why.
item.content:encoded: In a google-doc, as usual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me. The image you paint is very vivid and the anomaly is certainly bad news for concensus reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm liking where this is going so far, I kinda want to hear more about how a metaphysical/physical symbiote works, hopefully will learn more in future tales or skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because of horndoggo.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has very Tzeentch-like vibes (mutations, magic), I like it. Upvoting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Medium-length, creepy, memorable read. This is just what I wanted to see when this contest dropped. Great work, FuelTank.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent! I am a sucker for these kinds of skips and the fact that they actually seem somewhat benevolent (well, the ones that aren't committing murder. I mean that the skip itself isn't just "they turn into monsters that want to kill us all xdddd") and it's coming out of the site's best author for monsters! take my +1 you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Let's hope it's not a TPK for humanity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's really no one better than Cadaver at these sorts of monsters. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exclusion Zone 3396, where SCP-3396's central nexus is located, is currently contested by multiple organizations (including the Global Occult Coalition, the United Nations, and the United States' Unusual Incidents Unit) and as such has been declared a neutral demilitarized zone by mutual agreement to prevent global warfare and a Lifted Veil scenario.

Wouldn't the Hand also have their mitts in this as well? Either way upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new war begins. Stay tuned for the continuing story, featuring the Serpent’s Hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmhm.
The end of the world, just with a bunch of eigenweapons duking it out over various disagreements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like Mother 2.0

+1

item.title: there's something inside us
item.content:encoded:
the first of our doom offerings.

from here
https://pxhere.com/es/photo/1077809
CC0.

stand by, and see how this mutates ♥
